channel.title: Posts on Jung Yeon Park
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Jung Yeon Park

item.title: 1. Prepare offline kubeadm install
item.description: This is the first of three posts will focus on how to bring up a kubeadm cluster in an offline environment. I completed this a couple of months ago, when the latest k8s version was 1.9, but the concepts should translate over to newer versions. Prepare for kubernetes (*) Download required files on an Internet connected computer Initialize Kubeadm Kubeadm is one of the tools used to create a Kubernetes cluster.

item.title: Setting up Minikube with corporate proxy
item.description: Minikube is an easy way to get started with Kubernetes. It runs as a single-node Kubernetes cluster inside a VM, and includes almost all features of k8s, so you can try them out before installing them to an actual cluster. Supported addons include: Kubernetes dashboard EFK Registry Ingress and so on. (see https://github.com/kubernetes/minikube/blob/master/docs/addons.md) Minikube creates a VM that includes Kubernetes and a Docker daemon. To use containers inside this VM, you need to pass on the proxy settings to the docker daemon and install the certificate as well.
